echo ---SS---

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: openssl-toolbox
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app: openssl-toolbox
spec:
  containers:
    - name: openssl-toolbox
      image: alpine/openssl:latest
      command: ["sleep", "3600"]
  restartPolicy: Never
EOF
kubectl -n cockroachdb wait --for=condition=Ready pod/openssl-toolbox --timeout=60s

description:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: openssl-toolbox
  namespace: cockroachdb

EOF
kubectl -n cockroachdb wait --for=condition=Ready pod/openssl-toolbox --timeout=60s

summary:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: openssl-toolbox
  namespace: cockroachdb
  labels:
    app: openssl-toolbox
spec:
  containers:
    - name: openssl-toolbox
      image: alpine/openssl:latest
      command: ["sleep", "3600"]
  restartPolicy: Never
EOF
kubectl -n cockroachdb wait --for=condition=Ready pod/openssl-toolbox --timeout=60s

content.text: The openssl-toolbox pod is up. Now generating the CA and node/client certificates using CockroachDB's manual PKI approach.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- sh -c '
set -e
mkdir -p /work/ca /work/certs
cd /work

# 1. CA key + self-signed cert
openssl genrsa -out ca.key 2048
openssl req -new -x509 -days 3650 -key ca.key -out ca.crt -subj "/CN=Cockroach CA"

# 2. Node key + CSR with SANs for all pods + services + localhost
cat > node.cnf <<CNF
[req]
default_bits = 2048
prompt = no
distinguished_name = dn
req_extensions = v3_req
[dn]
CN = node
[v3_req]
basicConstraints = CA:FALSE
keyUsage = digitalSignature, keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = serverAuth, clientAuth
subjectAltName = @alt_names
[alt_names]
DNS.1 = localhost
DNS.2 = crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.3 = crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.4 = crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.5 = crdb-cluster
DNS.6 = crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.7 = crdb-cluster-public
DNS.8 = crdb-cluster-public.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
DNS.9 = *.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local
IP.1 = 127.0.0.1
CNF

openssl genrsa -out node.key 2048
openssl req -new -key node.key -out node.csr -config node.cnf
openssl x509 -req -in node.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out node.crt -days 3650 -extensions v3_req -extfile node.cnf

# 3. root client key + CSR
cat > client.cnf <<CNF
[req]
default_bits = 2048
prompt = no
distinguished_name = dn
req_extensions = v3_req
[dn]
CN = root
[v3_req]
basicConstraints = CA:FALSE
keyUsage = digitalSignature, keyEncipherment
extendedKeyUsage = clientAuth
CNF

openssl genrsa -out client.root.key 2048
openssl req -new -key client.root.key -out client.root.csr -config client.cnf
openssl x509 -req -in client.root.csr -CA ca.crt -CAkey ca.key -CAcreateserial \
  -out client.root.crt -days 3650 -extensions v3_req -extfile client.cnf

chmod 400 *.key
ls -la /work
'

content.text: Certs generated. Now pulling the material locally to build the Secret.

input.command:
mkdir -p /tmp/crdb-certs
for f in ca.crt node.crt node.key client.root.crt client.root.key; do
  kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /work/$f > /tmp/crdb-certs/$f
done
ls -la /tmp/crdb-certs
echo "---validate node cert SANs---"
openssl x509 -in /tmp/crdb-certs/node.crt -noout -text | grep -A2 "Subject Alternative Name" || true

content.content:
total 28
drwxr-xr-x 2 root root 4096 Jul  9 19:02 .
drwxrwxrwt 1 root root 4096 Jul  9 19:02 ..
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1123 Jul  9 19:02 ca.crt
-rw-r--r-- 1 root root 1147 Jul  9 19:02 client.root.crt

kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-cluster-certs \
  --from-file=ca.crt=/tmp/crdb-certs/ca.crt \
  --from-file=node.crt=/tmp/crdb-certs/node.crt \
  --from-file=node.key=/tmp/crdb-certs/node.key \
  --from-file=client.root.crt=/tmp/crdb-certs/client.root.crt \
  --from-file=client.root.key=/tmp/crdb-certs/client.root.key
kubectl -n cockroachdb get secret crdb-cluster-certs -o yaml | head -20

content.text: Now updating the StatefulSet to mount the certs and switch off insecure mode.
384 decimal = 0600 octal, so keys are owner-read-only which CockroachDB requires. Now watching the rollout.

content.text: The rolling update processes pods one at a time in reverse order, but CockroachDB can't run mixed TLS/insecure — node 2 now expects TLS while 0/1 are still insecure. I need to force all three to restart together onto the new revision.

kubectl -n cockroachdb delete pod crdb-cluster-0 crdb-cluster-1 crdb-cluster-2 --wait=false
sleep 5
kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -o wide
